auth.name: Your name
auth.mfaPrompt: Enter the 6-digit code from your authenticator app, or one of your recovery codes if you no longer have access to the device.
auth.mfaRecoveryWarning: Save these recovery codes now. Each one can be used once if you lose access to your authenticator. You will not see them again.
auth.mfaEnrollPrompt: Scan the QR code with your authenticator app (Google Authenticator, 1Password, Authy), then enter the 6-digit code it generates to confirm enrollment.
auth.mfaForcedEnrollmentExplanation: Your workspace requires every member to enroll a second factor before signing in.
auth.mfaAlreadyEnrolled: Two-factor authentication is already enabled on this account.
auth.mfaRecoveryCodesLowWarning: You are running low on recovery codes. Disable and re-enroll to generate a fresh set.
auth.mfaReEnrollInstruction: To replace your authenticator or generate new recovery codes, disable 2FA first.

folder.name: Name
folder.confidentialDesc: the server can read plaintext in memory during request handling, which is what enables previews, search, and virus scanning. Encrypted at rest. Not zero-knowledge.
folder.strictZkDesc: end-to-end encrypted, the server cannot decrypt this folder. You hold the keys. Previews, full-text search, and virus scanning are disabled.
folder.strictZkWarning: Strict-ZK is irreversible once the folder has content: there is no server-side migration path back to confidential mode because the server never had the plaintext to begin with.
folder.strictWarning: Strict-ZK is irreversible once the folder has content: there is no server-side migration path back to confidential mode because the server never had the plaintext to begin with.
folder.managedDescription: Confidential managed (recommended) — the server can read plaintext in memory during request handling, which is what enables previews, search, and virus scanning. Encrypted at rest. Not zero-knowledge.
folder.strictDescription: Strict zero-knowledge — end-to-end encrypted, the server cannot decrypt this folder. You hold the keys. Previews, full-text search, and virus scanning are disabled.

collab.readOnlyTooltip: You have view-only access to this document.
collab.disabledOther: {{mode}} is not allowed for this document's folder.
collab.markdownDesc: Headings, lists, basic text. Allowed in every privacy mode.
collab.richDesc: Tables, images, embeds. Requires confidential folders.
collab.richPresenceDesc: Live cursors and online users. Requires confidential folders.
collab.disabledStrictZk: {{mode}} requires the server to merge document updates, which is not possible in zero-knowledge folders where the server cannot read the document. Choose Markdown for this folder.
collab.disabledGeneric: {{mode}} is not allowed for this document's folder.

docs.deleteConfirm: Delete document "{{name}}"? This cannot be undone.
docs.empty: No documents in this folder yet. Click "New document" to start a collaborative editor session.
docs.modeFolderHint: The folder is {{mode}}. Only modes allowed by the folder's privacy boundary are selectable.
docs.disabledBanner: This document's collab mode is disabled — editing is locked.

search.fuzzyTooltip: Toggle trigram fuzzy matching (slower, finds typos)

encryption.title: Encryption
encryption.tooltipConfidential: Server-side encryption. Indexable, scannable, previewable. Encrypted at rest.
encryption.tooltipStrictZk: End-to-end encrypted. Server cannot decrypt. No previews, search, or scanning.
encryption.tooltipPublic: Encrypted at origin, served via expiring presigned URLs.
encryption.strictZkTooltip: Zero-knowledge: end-to-end encrypted. The server cannot decrypt this folder, so previews, full-text search, and virus scanning are disabled here.
encryption.confidentialTooltip: Confidential (managed encrypted storage): encrypted at rest, but the server can read plaintext in memory during request handling — which is what enables previews, full-text search, and virus scanning. NOT zero-knowledge.
encryption.clickToLearnMore: Click to learn more about privacy modes.
encryption.cmkSchemesHint: Accepted schemes: arn:aws:kms:..., kms://, vault://, transit://. Leave empty for the gateway default key.
encryption.modesExplanation: A CMK enables managed-encrypted folders where the gateway wraps per-object DEKs with the customer key — the server can still stream plaintext for preview, virus scan, and search. Strict-zero-knowledge folders remain end-to-end encrypted regardless of this setting; the server never sees plaintext there and all server-side processing is disabled.
encryption.defaultModeHeading: Default encryption mode for new folders
encryption.defaultModeDescription: Choose the encryption mode applied to new top-level folders by default. Members can still pick a mode per folder; this only sets the starting point. Existing folders are unaffected.
encryption.defaultModeManagedHint: New folders allow previews, full-text search, and virus scanning. Encrypted at rest; the server can read plaintext while handling requests.
encryption.defaultModeStrictHint: New folders are end-to-end encrypted by default — the server can never decrypt them, so previews, search, and scanning are disabled. Maximum privacy for sensitive workspaces.

privacy.intro: ZK Drive lets each folder pick one of two privacy modes. The choice is made when the folder is created and is surfaced everywhere a folder is shown (sidebar, file list, breadcrumb) via a coloured badge: <badgeManaged></badgeManaged> or <badgeStrict></badgeStrict>. We try to be honest about the trade-offs rather than market both modes as "zero-knowledge" — most providers do that and it is not accurate for either one.
privacy.brandHeading: What does "ZK" mean in ZK Drive?
privacy.brandBody: "ZK" describes a capability the platform offers, not a claim about every file you store. Every workspace ships with confidential managed storage by default — that is the right trade-off for most teams because it powers previews, search, and virus scanning. Strict zero-knowledge is opt-in at the folder level for the content where you want the server out of the loop entirely.
privacy.brandPointManaged: Confidential managed is encrypted at rest but server-readable in memory. It is the default. It is not zero-knowledge and we never call it that.
privacy.brandPointStrict: Strict zero-knowledge is end-to-end encrypted with keys the server never sees. Opt in per-folder. Losing the trade-offs (previews, search, virus scan) is the honest cost of the guarantee.
privacy.managedBody: Files are encrypted at rest by the zk-object-fabric storage gateway. The encryption keys live on the gateway, not on customer devices, so the server can read plaintext in memory during a request. That is what enables previews, full-text search, virus scanning, and admin recovery paths. This is the right default for most SMEs — but it is not zero-knowledge, and we say so plainly.
privacy.tableAdminRecovery: Admin recovery if a user loses access
privacy.managedServerRead: Yes, in memory during request handling
privacy.strictBody: Files are encrypted on your device with keys the server never sees. The gateway only ever stores opaque ciphertext for these folders. Because the server cannot decrypt the contents, previews, search, virus scanning, and admin password-reset recovery are all disabled for content in these folders — that is the honest cost of the guarantee. Choose this mode only for content where that trade-off is worth it.
privacy.strictVirus: Disabled (cannot scan what we cannot read)
privacy.strictAdminRecovery: Not possible — keys live on the client only
privacy.strictAtRest: Yes, end-to-end via the client SDK
privacy.strictReversibility: One-way: a folder cannot be downgraded back to managed once it has strict-ZK content, because the server never had the plaintext
privacy.pickingHeading: Which one should this folder be?
privacy.pickingDefault: Default to confidential managed for everyday documents, project workspaces, client deliverables, and anything you want to find by typing into search.
privacy.pickingStrict: Use strict zero-knowledge for legal hold, HR-investigation evidence, M&A diligence rooms, source code repos under NDA, customer credentials — anywhere losing the trade-off (previews, search, virus scan) is the right price for keeping the server out of the loop.
privacy.pickingPerFolder: Pick per folder, not per workspace. The choice is at the folder level so you can mix sensitive and non-sensitive content in the same workspace without giving up either mode.
privacy.auditBody: Both modes record the same audit metadata: who created the folder, when, parent folder, and the chosen privacy mode. File-level metadata (name, size, type, version, modification time, who shared it with whom) is also recorded in both modes — this is what lets share-links, retention policies, guest invitations, and billing work. For strict-zero-knowledge folders nothing else is recorded: no thumbnails, no extracted text, no scan verdict, no preview cache.
privacy.operatorNote: Operator note: the underlying storage layer is zk-object-fabric. Mode mapping: confidential managed = ManagedEncrypted; strict zero-knowledge = StrictZK. See the README and PROPOSAL §3.3 for the full threat model.

share.title: Share
share.fileNoParent: This file has no parent folder; invites must target a folder.
share.linkPasswordHint: Recipients must enter this password before opening the link.

billing.title: Billing
billing.adminOnlyDescription: Billing is restricted to workspace administrators.
billing.noPlanConfigured: No plan row configured — showing free-tier defaults.
billing.manageDescription: Pick a tier and start a Stripe Checkout flow, or open the customer portal to update payment details, swap tiers, or cancel.
billing.portalFailed: Could not open the customer portal
billing.bannerCheckoutSuccess: Checkout complete — your plan will update once Stripe delivers the webhook (usually within a few seconds).
billing.bannerCheckoutCancel: Checkout was cancelled. No changes were made to your plan.

starter.label: Starter
starter.blurb: 10 GB / user pooled, 25 users, 100 GB bandwidth / month.

business.label: Business
business.blurb: 1 TB pooled, 250 users, 1 TB bandwidth / month, audit log.

secure_business.label: Secure Business
secure_business.blurb: Customer-managed keys, regional placement, larger limits.

admin.title: Admin
admin.searchLanguageHint: Controls how the server stems and indexes file text for full-text search. Set to 'simple' for mixed-language or CJK workspaces; the trigram fallback always runs.
admin.adminOnlyDescription: This page is restricted to workspace administrators.

kchat.title: KChat rooms
kchat.syncDescription: The supplied list is the complete membership snapshot. Missing grants are revoked, new ones are added, and differing roles are updated.

preview.encryptedNoPreview: Preview unavailable for end-to-end encrypted files

install.title: Install zk-drive
install.description: Install the app for a faster, offline-capable experience.
install.bannerText: Install ZK Drive for quick access

notifications.enableTooltip: Get notified about share links and activity even when this tab is closed
notifications.blockedTooltip: Notifications are blocked in your browser settings for this site

errors.AUTH_INVALID_TOKEN: Your session has expired. Please sign in again.
errors.AUTH_REVOKED_TOKEN: Your session has been revoked. Please sign in again.
errors.AUTH_MISSING_IAT: This token is missing required claims. Please sign in again.
errors.AUTH_BAD_PURPOSE: This token is not valid for this action. Please sign in again.
errors.AUTH_REVOCATION_CHECK_FAILED: We couldn't verify your session. Please sign in again.
errors.RATE_LIMIT_EXCEEDED: You're doing that too often. Please try again later.
errors.AUTH_TOO_MANY_ATTEMPTS: Too many failed sign-in attempts from your network. Please wait a moment and try again.
errors.AUTH_SESSION_ANOMALY: We noticed a change in your device or network. Please sign in again to keep your account secure.
errors.FORBIDDEN: You don't have permission to do that.
errors.WRONG_TENANT: This resource belongs to a different workspace.
errors.PLATFORM_ADMIN_ACCESS_REQUIRED: This action requires platform administrator privileges.
errors.MALFORMED_JSON: The request body is not valid JSON.
errors.COLLAB_MODE_NOT_ALLOWED: This collaboration mode isn't allowed for this folder.
errors.UNSUPPORTED_SEARCH_LANGUAGE: That search language isn't supported. Pick one from the supported list.
errors.CONFLICT: This conflicts with the current state.
errors.GONE: This resource is no longer available.
errors.FILE_VIRUS_DETECTED: This file failed the virus scan.
errors.FABRIC_NOT_PROVISIONED: Workspace storage is not yet configured. Contact your administrator to provision storage.
errors.SHARE_PASSWORD_REQUIRED: This share link requires a password.
errors.SHARE_LINK_EXHAUSTED: This share link has reached its download limit.
errors.BILLING_NOT_CONFIGURED: Billing isn't set up for this workspace yet.
errors.STRIPE_NOT_CONFIGURED: Stripe billing isn't configured on this server. Contact your administrator to enable billing.
errors.INTERNAL_ERROR: Something went wrong on our end. Please try again.
